---
title: "geracao_energia_eletrica"
author: "Fernando Almeida Barbalho"
date: "2026-04-15"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(tidyverse)
library(colorspace)
library(ggrepel)
library(ggbreak)
library(cluster)
library(patchwork)

yearly_full_release_long_format <- 
  read_csv("yearly_full_release_long_format(1).csv") %>%
  janitor::clean_names()

theme_vpr<- function(paleta_cores){
  
  theme_light() +
    theme(
      panel.background = element_rect(fill= paleta_cores$cor_fundo_painel),
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "right",
      legend.background = element_rect(fill= paleta_cores$cor_fundo, color = paleta_cores$cor_fundo),
      #legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 8),
      plot.background = element_rect(fill =paleta_cores$cor_fundo ),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 8, face = "italic"),
      text = element_text( colour = paleta_cores$cor_texto),
      strip.background = element_rect(fill = paleta_cores$cor_fundo  ),
      strip.text = element_text(size= 7, face ="bold", color = paleta_cores$cor_texto)
    )  
  
  
}


paleta_cores<- list(  cor_fundo= "#D0D0D0", # "#E0E0E0", # # "#F8F8F8",
                      
                      cor_fundo_painel ="#F5F5F5",# "#D0D0D0",# "#2B2B2B",#  "#F5F5F5",
                      
                      cor_fundo_estados = "#696969",
                      
                      cor_fundo_mapa_pontos = "#2B2B2B", #"#505050"
                      
                      cor_texto =  "#324e5a" #"#F5F5F5"# 
)

```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r fig.dpi= 300}



# dados_graficos_dispersao_ee %>%
#   ggplot(aes(Clean, Fossil)) +
#   geom_path( aes(group = iso_3_code, color = iso_3_code) ,
#                   arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.2,"cm"), type = "closed"),
#              show.legend = FALSE,
#              size =1
#              ) + #color = "#4FC3F7"
#   # geom_point( fill= paleta_cores$cor_texto, pch= 21, color= paleta_cores$cor_fundo_painel, size =2, show.legend = FALSE) +
# 
#   geom_label_repel(data= dados_sel_ano_extremos, aes(label =paste0( ifelse(year %in% c(2020),as.character(year) ,"") ,ifelse(year %in% c(2020),": ",""), scales::number(t_wh, accuracy = 1.0, big.mark = "."),ifelse(year %in% c(2020)," TwH",""))),
#                   size =2.5,
#                   alpha = 0.8,
#                   #fontface = "bold",
#                   color = paleta_cores$cor_texto,
#                   show.legend = FALSE,
# ,                 label.padding = 0.1,
#                   ) +
#   scale_color_discrete_qualitative(palette = "Dark 2") +
#   facet_wrap(pais_2025~., scales= "free") +
#   theme_vpr(paleta_cores = paleta_cores) +
#   theme(
#     axis.text = element_text(size = 5),
#     axis.title = element_text(size = 6),
#     legend.position = "bottom",
#     legend.title = element_text(size = 8),
#     legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
#     panel.spacing = unit(0.01,"line"),
#     #legend.key.size = unit(0.8,"line")
#     legend.key.height = unit(0.4,"line")
#   ) +
#   labs(
#     fill = "Total generated energy in TwH",
#     size =  "Emissions in mtCO2/TWh",
#     x= "Clean sources in TwH",
#     y= "Fossil sources in TwH",
#     title = "Clean Power Rising, But Not Everywhere (2020-2025)", 
#     subtitle = str_wrap("Clean energy drives growth across major producers. China expands 21% in fossil and 62% in clean. France and Brazil remain low-emission benchmarks, while Canada, Germany, and Iran show setbacks.", 100),
#     caption = "Years from 2020 to 2025. Source: Ember. Elaboration by Fernando Barbalho"
#     
#   )

```


```{r fig.dpi= 300}

dados_emissao_geracao<-
  yearly_full_release_long_format %>%
  filter(category %in% c("Electricity generation", "Power sector emissions")  ,
         subcategory == "Aggregate fuel",
         variable %in% c("Clean", "Fossil"),
         between(year, 2020, 2025) ,
         !is.na(iso_3_code),
         unit %in% c("TWh", "mtCO2")) %>%
  summarise(total = sum(value, na.rm = TRUE),
            .by = c(year, iso_3_code, unit)) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = c(unit) ,
              values_from = total) %>%
  janitor::clean_names() %>%
  mutate(emission_per_mwh = mt_co2/t_wh) 




dados_geracao<-
  yearly_full_release_long_format %>%  
  filter(category %in% c("Electricity generation")  ,
         subcategory == "Aggregate fuel",
         variable %in% c("Clean", "Fossil"),
         between(year, 2020, 2025) ,
         !is.na(iso_3_code),
         unit %in% c("TWh")) %>%
  select(year, iso_3_code,variable, value) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = variable,
              values_from = value)

top_12_geradores_2025<-
  (dados_emissao_geracao %>%
  filter(year == 2025) %>%
  slice_max(n=12, order_by = t_wh))$iso_3_code


top_12_emissores_2025<-
  (dados_emissao_geracao %>%
  filter(year == 2025,
         iso_3_code %in% top_12_geradores_2025) %>%
  slice_max(n=12, order_by = emission_per_mwh))$iso_3_code




  
dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_previo <-
  dados_emissao_geracao %>%
  inner_join(dados_geracao) %>%
  filter(iso_3_code %in% top_12_geradores_2025) %>%
  mutate(year =as.factor(year),
         iso_3_code = factor(iso_3_code, levels =top_12_emissores_2025 )) 




dados_sel_ano<-
  dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_previo %>%
  filter(year %in% c(2025)) %>%
  mutate(pais_2025 = paste0(iso_3_code, " emissões em 2025: ", scales::number(emission_per_mwh, accuracy = 0.1, decimal.mark = ",", suffix = " mtCO2/TWh"))) %>%
  select(iso_3_code, pais_2025)

dados_sel_ano_extremos<-
  dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_previo %>%
  filter(year %in% c(2020,2025))

dados_graficos_dispersao_ee<-
  dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_previo %>%
  inner_join(dados_sel_ano) 



dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_soma <-
  dados_graficos_dispersao_ee %>%
  mutate(iso_3_code = ifelse(iso_3_code %in% c("CHN","USA"),as.character(iso_3_code),"Top países sem CHN e USA" )) %>%
  summarise(Clean  = sum(Clean),
            Fossil = sum(Fossil),
            t_wh = sum(t_wh),
            emission_per_mwh = sum(emission_per_mwh),
            .by = c(iso_3_code, year)) %>%
  bind_rows(
      dados_graficos_dispersao_ee %>%
        filter(iso_3_code == "BRA")
  )


dados_graf2<-
 dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_soma %>%
  filter(year %in% c(2020,2025)) %>%
  select(iso_3_code, year, emission_per_mwh) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = year,
              values_from = emission_per_mwh,
              names_prefix = "year_") %>%
  mutate(variacao = (year_2025/year_2020)-1) %>%
  mutate(iso_3_code = reorder(iso_3_code,variacao))


dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_soma$iso_3_code = factor(dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_soma$iso_3_code, levels =   sort(dados_graf2$iso_3_code) )

#dados_graf2$iso_3_code = factor(dados_graf2$iso_3_code, levels =  dados_graf2$iso_3_code)

  
dados_sel_ano_extremos_soma<-
  dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_soma %>%
  filter(year %in% c(2020,2025))


graf1<-
dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_soma %>%
  ggplot(aes(Clean, Fossil)) +
  geom_path( aes(group = iso_3_code, color = iso_3_code) ,
                  arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.1,"cm"), type = "closed"),
             show.legend = FALSE,
             #size =1
             ) + 
  geom_path(  data = dados_graficos_dispersao_ee, 
              aes(group = iso_3_code) ,
                  arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.1,"cm"), type = "closed"),
             show.legend = FALSE,
             color = "lightgray",
             alpha = 0.8,
             #size =1
             ) +
  geom_path( aes(group = iso_3_code, color = iso_3_code) ,
                  arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.1,"cm"), type = "closed"),
             show.legend = FALSE,
             #size =1
             ) +
  geom_text(data= dados_sel_ano_extremos_soma%>%filter(year==2025), 
            aes(label =  iso_3_code, color= iso_3_code), 
            show.legend = FALSE, 
            size =3, 
            vjust = -1,
            fontface = "bold") +
  geom_text(data= dados_sel_ano_extremos_soma%>%filter(iso_3_code=="USA"), 
            aes(label = paste0("year\n", year), color= iso_3_code,
                hjust = ifelse(year == 2020,1.1,-0.1 )), 
            show.legend = FALSE, 
            size =2)  +
  geom_text(data = tibble(Clean = 10, Fossil = 1800 ),
            aes(label = "Demais top 12"),
            hjust =0,
            size = 3,
             color = "lightgray",
             alpha = 0.8,
            show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(data = tibble(Clean = 800, Fossil = 60, iso_3_code = "BRA" ),
            aes(label = "Acréscimo de 18% na geração total \nAcréscimo de 25% em energia limpa",
                color = iso_3_code),
            vjust = 0,
            hjust =0,
            size = 3,
            show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(data = tibble(Clean = 4000, Fossil = 4000, iso_3_code = "Top países sem CHN e USA" ),
            aes(label = "Acréscimo de 9% na geração total\nAcréscimo de 13% em energia limpa",
                color = iso_3_code),
            vjust = 1,
            hjust =1,
            size = 3,
            show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(data = tibble(Clean = 2350, Fossil = 6200, iso_3_code = "CHN" ),
            aes(label = "Acréscimo de 34% na geração total\nAcréscimo de 63% em energia limpa",
                color = iso_3_code),
            vjust = 1,
            hjust =1,
            size = 3,
            show.legend = FALSE)  +
  geom_text(data = tibble(Clean = 2700, Fossil = 5000, iso_3_code = "CHN" ),
            aes(label = "Acréscimo da energia limpa (2024->2025) (520TwH) > acréscimo do\n grupo top países sem CHN e USA entre 2020 e 2025 (400TwH)",
                color = iso_3_code),
            vjust = 1,
            hjust =1,
            size = 3,
            show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_curve( data = tibble(Clean = 4000, Fossil = 6060, iso_3_code = "CHN"),
              aes( xend = 2750, yend = 4800, color = iso_3_code),
              curvature = -0.25, ncp = 50,
              arrow = arrow(length = unit(2, "mm"), type = "closed"),
              linetype = "dashed",
              show.legend = FALSE
  )   +
  geom_text(data = tibble(Clean = 1500, Fossil = 2450, iso_3_code = "USA" ),
            aes(label = "Acréscimo de 6% na geração total\nAcréscimo de 17% em energia limpa",
                color = iso_3_code),
            hjust =1,
            size = 3,
            show.legend = FALSE) +
  #scale_x_break(c(700, 1500)) + 
  #scale_x_break(c(1900, 2300)) + 
  #ggbreak::scale_y_break(c(1600, 2400)) + 
  #ggbreak::scale_y_break(c(2800, 3500)) +  #color = "#4FC3F7"
  scale_color_discrete_qualitative(palette = "Dark 2") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.08))) + 
  theme_vpr(paleta_cores = paleta_cores) +
  theme(
  ) +
  labs(
    x= "Clean sources in TWH",
    y= "Fossil sources in TWH",
    title = "Top 12 countries in energy generation", 
    subtitle = str_wrap("Clean energy drives growth across major producers. China 62% expands in clean and stabilizes in fossil. Brazil remains low-emission benchmark.", 100),
    caption = "Years from 2020 to 2025. Source: Ember. Elaboration by Fernando Barbalho"
    
  )

dados_graf2<-
  dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_soma %>%
  filter(year %in% c(2020,2025)) %>%
  select(iso_3_code, year, emission_per_mwh) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = year,
              values_from = emission_per_mwh,
              names_prefix = "year_") %>%
  mutate(variacao = (year_2025/year_2020)-1) %>%
  mutate()



graf2<-
  dados_graf2 %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=variacao, y=iso_3_code)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = iso_3_code ), show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(variacao, accuracy = 1.0)),
            hjust = 0,
            size =3) +
  scale_fill_discrete_qualitative(palette = "Dark 2") +
  theme_vpr(paleta_cores = paleta_cores) +
  theme(
    axis.text = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x = element_text(size = 6),
    axis.ticks = element_blank()
  )+ 
  labs(
    y="",
    x="Variation in mtCO2/TWh"
  )
  
graf1 + inset_element(graf2, l=.6, b=.05, r=.97, t=.4 )

```

```{r fig.dpi= 300}

# dados_modelo<- 
#   dados_geracao %>%
#   filter(year == 2025)
# 
# modelo_4<- cluster::pam(dados_modelo[,3:4], k=4)
# modelo_3<- cluster::pam(dados_modelo[,3:4], k=3)
# modelo_2<- cluster::pam(dados_modelo[,3:4], k=2)
# 
# 
# #summary(modelo_4)
# #summary(modelo_2)
# 
# dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_clusters<-
#   dados_graficos_dispersao_ee %>%
#   mutate( grupo = case_when(
#     iso_3_code == "CHN" ~"CHN",
#     iso_3_code == "USA" ~ "USA",
#     .default = "Rest of the world"
#   ) )
# 
# 
# 
# dados_graficos_dispersao_ee_clusters %>%
#   ggplot(aes(Clean, Fossil)) +
#   geom_path( aes(group = iso_3_code, color = iso_3_code) ,
#                   arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.1,"cm"), type = "open"),
#              show.legend = FALSE,
#              #size =1
#              ) + 
#   #scale_x_log10() +
#   #scale_y_log10() +
#   #scale_x_break(c(700, 1500)) + 
#   #scale_x_break(c(1900, 2300)) + 
#   #ggbreak::scale_y_break(c(1600, 2400)) + 
#   #ggbreak::scale_y_break(c(2600, 5100)) +  #color = "#4FC3F7"
# #   geom_point( fill= paleta_cores$cor_texto, pch= 21, color= paleta_cores$cor_fundo_painel, size =2, show.legend = FALSE) +
# # 
# #   geom_label_repel(aes(label =paste0( ifelse(year %in% c(2020),as.character(year) ,"") ,ifelse(year %in% c(2020),": ",""), scales::number(t_wh, accuracy = 1.0, big.mark = "."),ifelse(year %in% c(2020)," TwH",""))),
# #                   size =2.5,
# #                   alpha = 0.8,
# #                   #fontface = "bold",
# #                   color = paleta_cores$cor_texto,
# #                   show.legend = FALSE,
# # ,                 label.padding = 0.1,
# #                   ) +
# #   scale_color_discrete_qualitative(palette = "Dark 2") +
#   facet_wrap(grupo~., scales= "free") +
#   theme_vpr(paleta_cores = paleta_cores) +
#   theme(
#     axis.text = element_text(size = 5),
#     axis.title = element_text(size = 6),
#     legend.position = "bottom",
#     legend.title = element_text(size = 8),
#     legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
#     panel.spacing = unit(0.01,"line"),
#     #legend.key.size = unit(0.8,"line")
#     legend.key.height = unit(0.4,"line")
#   ) +
#   labs(
#     fill = "Total generated energy in TwH",
#     size =  "Emissions in mtCO2/TWh",
#     x= "Clean sources in TwH",
#     y= "Fossil sources in TwH",
#     title = "Clean Power Rising, But Not Everywhere (2020-2025)", 
#     subtitle = str_wrap("Clean energy drives growth across major producers. China expands 21% in fossil and 62% in clean. France and Brazil remain low-emission benchmarks, while Canada, Germany, and Iran show setbacks.", 100),
#     caption = "Years from 2020 to 2025. Source: Ember. Elaboration by Fernando Barbalho"
#     
#   )
# 



```
